title: One Coconut Tree
description: A counting song about the animals found around a coconut tree in St. Vincent and the Grenadines.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
One coconut tree, standin' tall and green,
[CLAP] One coconut tree, the best you've ever seen!
One bright parrot, flyin' round so free,
[SNAP] One bright parrot, happy as can be!

[Chorus]
One, one, de ting go so!
[STOMP] One, one, watch the rhythm flow!
Island vibes, feel the sun,
Counting fun for everyone!

[Verse 2]
Two little lizards, sunning on a stone,
[CLAP] Two little lizards, makin' St. Vincent home!
Two yellow butterflies, dancin' in the breeze,
[SNAP] Two yellow butterflies, among the mango trees!

[Chorus]
One, one, de ting go so!
[STOMP] One, one, watch the rhythm flow!
Island vibes, feel the sun,
Counting fun for everyone!

[Verse 3]
Three little crabs, crawlin' on the sand,
[CLAP] Three little crabs, a happy island band!
Three brown mongoose, searchin' for a treat,
[SNAP] Three brown mongoose, quick upon their feet!

[Chorus]
One, one, de ting go so!
[STOMP] One, one, watch the rhythm flow!
Island vibes, feel the sun,
Counting fun for everyone!

[Verse 4]
Four hummingbirds flyin' - "leh we go!" (let's go!)
[CLAP] Four hummingbirds near the flower row!
Four green iguanas, lookin' oh so fine,
[SNAP] Four green iguanas, takin' all their time!

[Chorus]
One, one, de ting go so!
[STOMP] One, one, watch the rhythm flow!
Island vibes, feel the sun,
Counting fun for everyone!
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant, cartoon-style illustration for a Caribbean children's song. The scene is a sunny beach in St. Vincent, with a tall, green coconut tree as the centerpiece. Around the tree, there are cartoon animals: one bright parrot, two little lizards, three crabs, and four hummingbirds. Children with smiling faces are dancing and playing musical instruments like steelpan drums and maracas. The background features clear blue water and lush green hills. The overall style should be colorful, cheerful, and inviting, reflecting the energy of Caribbean music.
educationalValue: This song teaches children to count from one to four and introduces them to animals found in St. Vincent and the Grenadines. It also uses Patois, expanding their understanding of different dialects and cultures. Kids will also develop body awareness through the use of [CLAP],[SNAP],[STOMP] and understand that learning and diversity is fun!
